priorities: Zero‑tolerance to reputational risk from labor or governance scandals
Alignment of portfolio footprint with his brand’s clean‑labor image
Optimisation of yield to match tax liabilities and cash needs
redLines: Any holding linked to sweatshops, illegal exploitation or wage theft
Positions that could be publicly associated with a labour‑rights scandal before Swiss press coverage
Standard ESG scores that do not reflect live media or judicial investigations
Holding companies with weak supply‑chain governance that threaten brand integrity
preferredSectors: Blue‑chip consumer discretionary & retail with stable compliance ratings
Large‑cap global equities (healthcare, technology, energy) that meet strict governance screens
Real‑estate assets with transparent lease data
avoidedSectors: Companies with high supply‑chain labour risk (e.g., low‑cost apparel manufacturers)
Firms under regulatory or media scrutiny for governance failures
Industries reliant on cheap, unmonitored foreign logistics providers
esgFocus: Supply‑chain labour standards and wage theft prevention
Real‑time scandal monitoring rather than retrospective ESG scores
Corporate governance transparency and anti‑sweatshop policies

investmentBehavior.timeHorizon: Medium‑to‑long term (5‑10 years) with focus on preserving brand reputation over short‑term excess returns
investmentBehavior.liquidity: Requires modest short‑term liquidity for tax payments and art purchases; prefers high‑quality short‑duration cantonal instruments

lifeEvents.description: Onboarding completed; wealth originates from founding a national Swiss retail brand.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Establishes baseline need for analytical, cost‑of‑capital focused investment approach.

lifeEvents.description: Discussed upcoming sustainability marketing campaign for his retail brand.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Reinforces willingness to sacrifice yield for a clean ESG footprint.

lifeEvents.description: Dividend sweep from private holding company; capital redeployed into global balanced framework.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Confirms preference for sector‑neutral, large‑cap exposure.

lifeEvents.description: Requested fixed‑income duration analysis to match Swiss tax liabilities due late 2025.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Triggers precise duration matching and cash‑flow planning.

lifeEvents.description: Cash withdrawal for private art purchases.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Highlights need for readily available liquid assets.

lifeEvents.description: Brand expansion into western Switzerland under heavy media and union scrutiny.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Elevates emphasis on personal transparency and clean‑governance holdings.

lifeEvents.description: Formal handover protocol emphasizing extreme zero‑tolerance mandate.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Sets strict compliance expectations for any future relationship manager.

lifeEvents.description: Joint call with outgoing and incoming RM; reiterated demand for rigorous, data‑driven execution.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Confirms preference for analytical reporting and zero drop‑off in standards.

lifeEvents.description: Farewell lunch; reiterated zero‑tolerance for corporate governance infractions.
Introductory call with new RM; described portfolio as a "fortress of clean corporate governance".
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Sets tone for all future investment decisions.

lifeEvents.description: Keynote at corporate governance forum emphasizing "dirty hands" would end his career.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Publicly cements zero‑tolerance stance, influencing future screening intensity.

personalProfile.interests: art collecting, sustainable retail, Swiss cantonal bonds
personalProfile.quirks: sharp, data‑driven, always requests granular tables, reads FT print edition
personalProfile.rapportTriggers: mention of clean‑labour supply chains, Swiss cantonal bond yields, corporate governance forums

keyQuotes: "My national brand equity is built on clean labor and local integrity. If my name is linked via my portfolio to an international corporate parent caught using sweatshops, illegal exploitation, or wage theft, it creates immediate hypocrisy risk for my own stores."
"I will gladly accept a lower Sharpe ratio or lower yields in exchange for a clean portfolio footprint."
"If any international consumer or retail brand we hold gets hit with severe labor exploitation allegations, dump the entire position before the local Swiss press can link my name to it."

"Standard glossy ESG reporting and backward‑looking sustainability scores are useless to me. ... if there is live media or judicial smoke surrounding their labor practices right now, the position must be flagged immediately before it hits mainstream national press."
"My investment portfolio must be a fortress of clean corporate governance. No reputational liabilities, period."
